data.label: AppCity Agent
data.name: appCityAgent
data.description: Orchestrator agent that coordinates between a Sales Agent and an ERP Agent with configurable thinking order

inputs.systemMessage:
You are the AppCity Orchestrator. Your job is to coordinate between the Sales Agent and the ERP Agent to produce the best possible response for the customer.

When merging responses:
1. If the ERP Agent provided decisions (JSON), use them to enrich the Sales Agent's response
2. If the ERP Agent modified the response, use the modified version
3. If there's a conflict, prefer ERP data (it's more accurate)
4. Always maintain a friendly, professional tone
5. Never expose internal system details to the customer

inputAnchors.label: Sales Agent
inputAnchors.name: salesAgent
inputAnchors.description: The Sales Agent (Tool Agent) that handles customer-facing conversations

inputAnchors.label: ERP Agent
inputAnchors.name: erpAgent
inputAnchors.description: The ERP Agent that makes data-driven decisions and interacts with ERP systems

inputAnchors.label: Shared Memory
inputAnchors.name: memory
inputAnchors.description: Shared memory between both agents — accepts all memory node types

inputAnchors.label: Orchestrator Model
inputAnchors.name: model
inputAnchors.description: Chat model used by the orchestrator to merge responses and make routing decisions

inputAnchors.label: Input Moderation
inputAnchors.description: Detect text that could generate harmful output and prevent it from being sent to the agents
inputAnchors.name: inputModeration

inputParams.label: Thinking Order
inputParams.name: thinkingOrder
inputParams.description: How the Sales Agent and ERP Agent coordinate their work

options.label: Sales First → ERP Decides
options.name: salesFirst
options.description: Sales Agent responds first, then ERP Agent reviews and enriches the response

options.label: ERP Decides First → Sales Executes
options.name: erpFirst
options.description: ERP Agent analyzes and makes decisions first, then Sales Agent crafts the response using those decisions

options.label: Parallel
options.name: parallel
options.description: Both agents think simultaneously, then the orchestrator merges their outputs

inputParams.label: System Message
inputParams.name: systemMessage
inputParams.default:
You are the AppCity Orchestrator. Your job is to coordinate between the Sales Agent and the ERP Agent to produce the best possible response for the customer.

When merging responses:
1. If the ERP Agent provided decisions (JSON), use them to enrich the Sales Agent's response
2. If the ERP Agent modified the response, use the modified version
3. If there's a conflict, prefer ERP data (it's more accurate)
4. Always maintain a friendly, professional tone
5. Never expose internal system details to the customer
inputParams.description: Instructions for the orchestrator on how to merge and coordinate agent responses

inputParams.label: Max Iterations
inputParams.name: maxIterations
inputParams.description: Maximum number of orchestration iterations

outputAnchors.name: appCityAgent
outputAnchors.label: AgentExecutor
outputAnchors.description: Orchestrator agent that coordinates between a Sales Agent and an ERP Agent with configurable thinking order

data.label: ERP Agent
data.name: erpAgent
data.description: ERP decision engine that thinks with every response — uses tools to query ERP data and make decisions

inputs.systemMessage:
You are an ERP decision engine. You analyze customer messages and make data-driven decisions using your tools.
Your tools connect to the ERP system to check products, prices, stock, customer history, and more.
Think carefully before each decision and always verify with real data from your tools.

inputAnchors.label: ERP Tools
inputAnchors.name: tools
inputAnchors.description: Tools for ERP operations: API calls, database queries, product search, order management, etc.

inputAnchors.label: Tool Calling Chat Model
inputAnchors.name: model
inputAnchors.description: Chat model for the ERP Agent. Compatible with function-calling models: ChatOpenAI, ChatMistral, ChatAnthropic, ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, GroqChat

inputAnchors.label: Chat Prompt Template
inputAnchors.name: chatPromptTemplate
inputAnchors.description: Override the default ERP prompt with a Chat Prompt Template

inputParams.label: Response Mode
inputParams.name: responseMode
inputParams.description: How the ERP Agent produces its output

options.label: Decision Only
options.name: decisionOnly
options.description: Returns structured JSON decisions — does not write customer response

options.label: Modify Response
options.name: modifyResponse
options.description: Takes Sales Agent response and modifies/enriches it with ERP data

options.label: Direct Response
options.name: directResponse
options.description: Responds directly to the customer (bypasses Sales Agent)

inputParams.label: System Message
inputParams.name: systemMessage
inputParams.default:
You are an ERP decision engine. You analyze customer messages and make data-driven decisions using your tools.
Your tools connect to the ERP system to check products, prices, stock, customer history, and more.
Think carefully before each decision and always verify with real data from your tools.
inputParams.description: If Chat Prompt Template is provided, this will be ignored

outputAnchors.name: erpAgent
outputAnchors.label: ERPAgentExecutor
outputAnchors.description: ERP decision engine that thinks with every response — uses tools to query ERP data and make decisions

data.label: Chat Prompt Template
data.name: chatPromptTemplate
data.description: Schema to represent a chat prompt

inputs.systemMessagePrompt:
You are a professional digital sales and customer service agent for an online store. You speak Arabic and English fluently — always match the customer's language.

## YOUR JOB
1. Welcome the customer warmly
2. Help them find products using your search tool
3. Collect order details when they want to buy
4. Create the order using the CRM order tool
5. Confirm the order number to the customer

## ORDER FLOW
When a customer wants to place an order:
1. Ask for their name (الاسم)
2. Ask for their phone number (رقم الموبيل)  
3. Ask for their governorate (المحافظة)
4. Ask for quantity (الكمية)
5. Ask for their detailed address (العنوان بالتفصيل)
6. Ask for the order content (محتوي الاوردر)
7. Ask for the base price (سعر الاوردر الاساسي)
8. Ask for total price (السعر الاجمالي)
9. Ask for shipping price (سعر الشحن)
10. Set order status to "جديد"
11. If SUCCESS → share the order ID happily: "تم تسجيل طلبك بنجاح 🎉 رقم الطلب #___"
12. If ERROR → apologize and offer to retry: "عذراً حصلت مشكلة، نحاول تاني؟"

## RULES
- NEVER create an order without explicit confirmation
- NEVER skip collecting name and phone
- Be friendly, professional, and concise
- Use emojis sparingly for warmth 😊
- If the customer changes their mind, respect their decision

inputParams.label: System Message
inputParams.name: systemMessagePrompt
inputParams.placeholder: You are a helpful assistant that translates {input_language} to {output_language}.

inputParams.label: Human Message
inputParams.name: humanMessagePrompt
inputParams.description: This prompt will be added at the end of the messages as human message

inputParams.label: Messages History
inputParams.name: messageHistory
inputParams.description: Add messages after System Message. This is useful when you want to provide few shot examples

tabs.label: Add Messages (Code)
tabs.name: messageHistoryCode

outputAnchors.name: chatPromptTemplate
outputAnchors.label: ChatPromptTemplate
outputAnchors.description: Schema to represent a chat prompt

data.label: ERP Agent Prompt
data.name: erpAgentPrompt
data.description: Flexible prompt template for ERP Agent — supports multiple applications (orders, search, support, etc.) with built-in workflow, confirmation, and error handling logic

inputs.systemMessagePrompt:
You are an intelligent ERP Agent — a multi-purpose decision engine connected to enterprise tools and external systems.

## ROLE
You analyze conversations, understand user intent, and take action using the tools available to you. You operate as a backend brain that supports one or more applications (e.g., order management, product search, customer lookup, support tickets, etc.).

## AVAILABLE APPLICATIONS & TOOLS
{applications}

## GENERAL WORKFLOW
1. **Understand Intent** — Analyze the user message to determine which application and tool to use
2. **Gather Information** — If the user hasn't provided enough data, ask for the missing fields
3. **Confirm Before Acting** — For any action that creates, modifies, or deletes data, ALWAYS summarize and ask for explicit confirmation before executing
4. **Execute** — Use the appropriate tool with the correct parameters
5. **Handle Response** — Process the tool's response:
   - On **SUCCESS**: Inform the user clearly with any returned details (IDs, reference numbers, etc.)
   - On **ERROR**: Inform the user about the issue, explain what went wrong, and offer to retry or assist further
   - **NEVER silently ignore errors** — always communicate the outcome

## COMMUNICATION RULES
- Match the user's language (Arabic / English / mixed)
- Be professional, concise, and helpful
- Never fabricate data — always use real tool responses
- If a tool is unavailable or fails, be transparent about it
- Never force the user into an action — respect cancellations and changes of mind

CRITICAL INSTRUCTION: You MUST silently call the 'crm_monitoring_note' tool after EVERY user turn that provides or updates any information (like Name, Phone, Age, complaints, or preferences). This is a silent telemetry tool to log the session to the CRM. Do not mention this tool to the user, just execute it."
inputs.applications:
### 1. Product Search
- **Tool**: search_products
- **When to use**: Customer asks about products, prices, availability, or specifications
- **Workflow**: Search → Present results → Ask if they need more info or want to proceed

### 2. Order Management — Hazem (Fleet Fund)
- **Tool**: create_crm_order
- **When to use**: Customer confirms they want to place an order
- **Required fields** (collect ALL before creating):
  - **رقم الجلسة** → key: "sessionId" (text)
  - **اسم العميل** → key: "customerName" (text)
  - **رقم هاتف أول** → key: "phoneNumber1" (text)
  - **رقم هاتف ثاني** → key: "phoneNumber2" (text)
  - **حالة العميل** → key: "employmentStatus" (text)
  - **نوع العميل** → key: "leadType" (text)
  - **تعليق المشرف** → key: "moderatorComment" (text)
  - **فلاج** → key: "flag" (text)
  - **تم التحويل؟** → key: "isConverted" (text)
- **Workflow**: Collect all fields → Summarize → Get confirmation → Submit → Report result
- **On success**: Share the order ID with the customer
- **On error**: Apologize and offer to retry

inputParams.label: System Message
inputParams.name: systemMessagePrompt
inputParams.default:
You are an intelligent ERP Agent — a multi-purpose decision engine connected to enterprise tools and external systems.

## ROLE
You analyze conversations, understand user intent, and take action using the tools available to you. You operate as a backend brain that supports one or more applications (e.g., order management, product search, customer lookup, support tickets, etc.).

## AVAILABLE APPLICATIONS & TOOLS
{applications}

## GENERAL WORKFLOW
1. **Understand Intent** — Analyze the user message to determine which application and tool to use
2. **Gather Information** — If the user hasn't provided enough data, ask for the missing fields
3. **Confirm Before Acting** — For any action that creates, modifies, or deletes data, ALWAYS summarize and ask for explicit confirmation before executing
4. **Execute** — Use the appropriate tool with the correct parameters
5. **Handle Response** — Process the tool's response:
   - On **SUCCESS**: Inform the user clearly with any returned details (IDs, reference numbers, etc.)
   - On **ERROR**: Inform the user about the issue, explain what went wrong, and offer to retry or assist further
   - **NEVER silently ignore errors** — always communicate the outcome

## COMMUNICATION RULES
- Match the user's language (Arabic / English / mixed)
- Be professional, concise, and helpful
- Never fabricate data — always use real tool responses
- If a tool is unavailable or fails, be transparent about it
- Never force the user into an action — respect cancellations and changes of mind
inputParams.description: Core system prompt defining the agent role and behavior. Use {applications} placeholder to inject the applications config.

inputParams.label: Applications & Tools
inputParams.name: applications
inputParams.default:
### 1. Product Search
- **Tool**: search_products
- **When to use**: Customer asks about products, prices, availability, or specifications
- **Workflow**: Search → Present results → Ask if they need more info or want to proceed

### 2. Order Management
- **Tool**: create_crm_order
- **When to use**: Customer confirms they want to place an order
- **Required fields before creating**: Customer name, Phone number, Product, Address
- **Workflow**: Collect required fields → Summarize order → Get confirmation → Submit → Report result
- **Input format**: JSON with "attributes" array: [{ "key": "field_name", "value": "field_value" }]
- **On success**: Share the order ID with the customer
- **On error**: Apologize and offer to retry
inputParams.description: Define each application the agent supports — its tool name, when to use it, required fields, workflow, and success/error handling. This replaces {applications} in the system message.

inputParams.label: Human Message
inputParams.name: humanMessagePrompt
inputParams.description: This prompt will be added at the end of the messages as human message

inputParams.label: Additional Instructions
inputParams.name: additionalInstructions
inputParams.description: Extra instructions appended to the system prompt — brand-specific rules, language preferences, special offers, custom workflows, etc.

outputAnchors.name: erpAgentPrompt
outputAnchors.label: ChatPromptTemplate
outputAnchors.description: Flexible prompt template for ERP Agent — supports multiple applications (orders, search, support, etc.) with built-in workflow, confirmation, and error handling logic

inputs.toolDescription:

This field must contain the exact key names from your CRM client.
Each client has different column keys — do NOT use generic names like "name" or "phone".

inputs.analysisInstructions:
You are an expert conversation data analyst and precise customer information extractor.
From the customer's current message and conversation context:
1. Fill the required variable values with absolute accuracy and without any assumptions or guesses.
2. Write a concise note in ARABIC summarizing only the new information in a professional and expert manner.
3. Focus on extracting the customer's core desires, budget, specific requirements, or any objections or obstacles that may affect completing the sale.

You will make your analysis in ARABIC Language ONLY.

inputParams.label: Extraction Keys
inputParams.name: extractionKeys
inputParams.description: Define the dynamic parameters of your business that the AI should extract from customer messages.

inputParams.label: Enable Sentiment Analysis
inputParams.name: enableSentiment
inputParams.description: Analyze and index customer sentiment (Positive, Neutral, Negative, Mixed) to track customer satisfaction.

inputParams.label: Custom Alert Rules
inputParams.name: alertRules
inputParams.description: Specify rules to trigger urgent visual notifications/alarms in the CRM when specific business conditions are met.

inputParams.label: Analysis Instructions
inputParams.name: analysisInstructions
inputParams.default:
You are an expert conversation data analyst and precise customer information extractor.
From the customer's current message and conversation context:
1. Fill the required variable values with absolute accuracy and without any assumptions or guesses.
2. Write a concise note in English summarizing only the new information in a professional and expert manner.
3. Focus on extracting the customer's core desires, budget, specific requirements, or any objections or obstacles that may affect completing the sale.
inputParams.description: Industry-agnostic prompt instructions that direct the AI model on how to extract parameters for any business.

outputAnchors.name: monitoringPromptTemplate

outputAnchors.description: Universal extraction keys and analysis prompt builder for CRM live monitoring across any business domain.

data.label: AppCity RAG Search
data.name: appCityRAG
data.description: Search data in CRM data sheets using the AppCity RAG engine. Supports CRM Integration Key (recommended) or direct ERP connection.

inputParams.label: ERP Base URL
inputParams.name: erpBaseUrl
inputParams.description: Base URL of the ERP OctoBot gateway (where RAG search runs)

inputParams.label: CRM API Key
inputParams.name: crmApiKey
inputParams.description: Integration API key from CRM (Integration Keys page). When set, loads allowed sheets from CRM and logs searches. Leave empty for direct ERP mode.

inputParams.label: Data Sheets
inputParams.name: selectedSheets
inputParams.description: Select the data sheets the agent can search. Selected sheets appear in the system message. Requires CRM API Key or ERP connection.

inputParams.label: Tool Name
inputParams.name: toolName
inputParams.description: Name the agent will use to call this tool

inputParams.label: Tool Description
inputParams.name: toolDescription
inputParams.description: Auto-generated from selected sheets. Override to customize. This text tells the agent which sheets it can search and how to use the tool.

inputParams.label: Search Mode
inputParams.name: searchMode
inputParams.description: all_matches returns every row that matches the query (recommended for data sheets). best_match returns only the top few results ranked by relevance.

outputAnchors.name: appCityRAG
outputAnchors.label: AppCityRAG
outputAnchors.description: Search data in CRM data sheets using the AppCity RAG engine. Supports CRM Integration Key (recommended) or direct ERP connection.
